about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: A bit about myself
about.bio: I'm a Master's student in Computer Science at the University of L'Aquila, specializing in the AICONDA track (AI, Computing and Data Analytics). I graduated with honors, with a thesis on the computational complexity of Nine Men's Morris. I'm passionate about artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and web development, and I enjoy challenging myself by solving algorithmic problems.

projects.title: Projects & Competitions
projects.subtitle: A selection of my most significant work

items.title: Flood Monitoring and Management System
items.description: A system for monitoring and preventing environmental disasters, developed during the CINI Challenge at iCities. Winner of the SellaLab award offered by Banca Sella.

items.title: CyberChallenge 2024
items.description: Participation in the national cybersecurity competition, reaching the final stage in Turin.

experiences.description: Participant in CyberChallenge 2024, reaching the national stage in Turin and gaining hands-on cybersecurity skills.
Development of the "Flood Monitoring and Management System" for environmental disaster monitoring and prevention. Winner of the SellaLab award offered by Banca Sella.
Organization and management of the computer science pop-up stand and the Informatics Olympiad for high school students.

education.degree: Master's Degree in Computer Science — AICONDA Track
education.description: Specialization in AI, Computing and Data Analytics.
Thesis: "Study of the Computational Complexity of Nine Men's Morris". Skills in analysis, software design, programming languages and teamwork.
